column1: # dynamic-delivery-workflow — sample wave queue (TAB-separated, dependency-first order).
# Copy this into your control dir as queue.tsv and edit. The scripts read it row by row.
# Lines starting with # and blank lines are ignored.
# Columns (TAB-separated — NOT spaces; a single literal tab between each field):
#   ISSUE        GitHub issue number. Drives `gh issue view`, the branch name, and the worktree dir.
#   TAG          Short slug used in the branch name + to locate the brief (e.g. PAY-19, AUTH-03).
#                Branch = <BRANCH_PREFIX>/<TAG>-<ISSUE>.
#   TIER         1 = prerequisite (its branch is merged INTO the integration base before any
#                dependent runs, so dependents build on it); 2 = ordinary (PR only, no base-merge).
#   TIER1_MERGE  yes = after this issue's PR is created, fast-merge its branch into the integration
#                base via the _integration worktree. Only meaningful for TIER 1. Use `no` otherwise.
#   DEPS         comma-separated ISSUE numbers that must be merged into base first ( - = none).
#                A dependent DEFERS until every dep is in base history, then is picked up on a re-loop.
#   BRIEF_GLOB   filename (or glob) of the per-issue brief under ${BRIEF_DIR} (set in wave.conf).
#                The brief is the authoritative spec handed to Codex alongside the issue body.
#   TIMEOUT_MIN  per-codex-attempt hard timeout in MINUTES. Adaptive — do NOT use one flat value:
#                  ~75 for backend/feature issues, ~40 default, ~20 for docs-only.
#                v1's flat 40 killed 3 big features (exit 124); size this per row.
# ISSUE  TAG     TIER  TIER1_MERGE  DEPS     BRIEF_GLOB                          TIMEOUT_MIN
# --- TIER 1 (prereqs; merged into base on pass, in this order) ---
# --- TIER 2 (rest; PR into base, no auto-merge into base) ---
